name: Dharmaraja Adhvarindra
dates_notes: Late 16th – early 17th c.; dated from manuscripts and citations.
core_thesis: Dharmarāja Adhvarīndra is the great pedagogical systematiser of Vivaraṇa-Advaita epistemology. Working downstream of Citsukha and Madhusūdana, he organises the Advaitic theory of *pramāṇas* into a single, teachable architecture in the technical idiom of pre-Gaṅgeśa and early-Navya Nyāya. His distinctive doctrines: (i) Advaita accepts the six Bhāṭṭa *pramāṇas* — *pratyakṣa, anumāna, upamāna, śabda, arthāpatti, anupalabdhi* — at the *vyāvahārika* level; (ii) *pratyakṣa* is analysed as the *antaḥkaraṇa-vṛtti*'s contact, via the sense-streams, with the object, with the consciousness underlying object (*viṣaya-caitanya*) and consciousness underlying *vṛtti* (*vṛtti-caitanya*) becoming non-different — the famous *vṛtti-vyāpti* analysis; (iii) the Mahāvākya alone is *brahma-pramāṇa*, generating the *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* whose function is to remove *avidyā* though knowledge itself is never produced; (iv) sustained refutation of the Naiyāyika analyses of *pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, and erroneous cognition. He is firmly within the *Vivaraṇa* line on the locus-of-*avidyā* question and writes downstream of Citsukha and Madhusūdana.

engaged_works.title: Vedanta-Paribhasha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; the standard manual of Advaita epistemology.
engaged_works.summary: The standard manual (*paribhāṣā* — 'technical-vocabulary handbook') of Advaita epistemology, organised in eight *paricchedas* — one per *pramāṇa* (*pratyakṣa, anumāna, upamāna, āgama* / *śabda, arthāpatti, anupalabdhi*) plus *prameya* and *prayojana*. Dharmarāja gives the canonical Advaitic *vṛtti-vyāpti* analysis of perception (the *antaḥkaraṇa* extends through the senses, conforms to the object's shape, and consciousness underlying *vṛtti* becomes non-different from consciousness underlying object); develops the Advaita treatment of *anumāna* in the language of pre-Gaṅgeśa and early Navya-Nyāya; gives the standard exposition of the Mahāvākya as *brahma-pramāṇa* generating the *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti*; and formulates the Advaita doctrine of *prātibhāsika*, *vyāvahārika*, and *pāramārthika* levels of reality. The work is the universal entry-point to technical Advaita epistemology in modern *Vedānta* curricula and one of the most-commented texts of late Advaita; Rāmakṛṣṇa Adhvarī's *Śikhāmaṇi* (with the *Maṇiprabhā* sub-commentary) is the standard apparatus.
